```{.python code-line-numbers="1-5|7-19"}
    def __init__(self, val=0, nxt=None):
        self.val = val; self.next = nxt

def merge_k_lists(lists):
    heap = []
    # seed with head of each list
    for i, node in enumerate(lists):
        if node:
            heapq.heappush(heap, (node.val, i, node))

    dummy = cur = ListNode(0)
    while heap:
        val, i, node = heapq.heappop(heap)
        cur.next = node
        cur      = cur.next
        if node.next:
            heapq.heappush(heap, (node.next.val, i, node.next))

    return dummy.next
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    subgraph "3 sorted lists → min-heap"
        L1["List 1: 1→4→7"]
        L2["List 2: 2→5→8"]
        L3["List 3: 3→6→9"]
    end
    HEAP["Min-Heap<br/>[(1,L1),(2,L2),(3,L3)]"]
    HEAP --> POP1["pop 1 → push 4<br/>[(2,L2),(3,L3),(4,L1)]"]
    POP1 --> POP2["pop 2 → push 5<br/>[(3,L3),(4,L1),(5,L2)]"]
    POP2 --> OUT(["Output: 1→2→3→4→5→6→7→8→9"])
    classDef step fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef out  fill:#1a6b3c,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class L1,L2,L3,HEAP,POP1,POP2 step
    class OUT out
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
| Operation | Time |
|---|---|
| Build initial heap | O(K) |
| Each element | O(log K) |
| **Total** | **O(N log K)** |

*N = total elements across all lists*
:::
:::
:::

---

## Pattern Recognition Cheatsheet {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Signal → Pattern

| Signal words | Pattern |
|---|---|
| sorted array + pair/triplet | Two Pointers |
| subarray / substring, contiguous | Sliding Window |
| range sum, subarray sum = k | Prefix Sum |
| linked list cycle / middle | Fast & Slow Pointers |
| reverse a linked list | In-place Reversal |
| K largest / K most frequent | Top-K + Heap |
| stream median | Two Heaps |
| overlapping intervals | Merge Intervals |
| range [1..n], missing/dup | Cyclic Sort |
| next greater / smaller | Monotonic Stack |
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Signal → Pattern (continued)

| Signal words | Pattern |
|---|---|
| sorted/rotated, find condition | Modified Binary Search |
| level by level, BFS on tree | Tree BFS |
| path sum, depth, LCA | Tree DFS |
| prerequisites, build order | Topological Sort |
| connected components, union | Union-Find |
| take/skip item, subset sum | 0/1 Knapsack DP |
| all permutations/subsets/combos | Backtracking |
| K sorted lists, K-way merge | K-way Merge Heap |

::: {.fragment}
::: {.callout-tip}
Read the problem → identify signal words → pick pattern → adapt template → optimise.
:::
:::
:::
:::

---

## Interview & Practice — Overview {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'flowchart': {'rankSpacing': 55, 'nodeSpacing': 35}}}%%
graph LR
    INT["🎯 DSA Interview<br/>& Practice"] --> PREP["📚 Preparation<br/>Strategy"]
    INT --> SOLVE["🧩 Problem-Solving<br/>Framework"]
    INT --> TYPES["📋 Question<br/>Categories"]
    INT --> TIPS["💡 Tips &<br/>Common Mistakes"]
    INT --> PRAC["🏋️ Practice<br/>Resources"]
    PREP --> P1["Study by Pattern<br/>not by problem"]
    PREP --> P2["Spaced Repetition<br/>revisit weekly"]
    SOLVE --> S1["UMPIRE Method"]
    SOLVE --> S2["Complexity<br/>Analysis habit"]
    TYPES --> T1["Easy / Medium / Hard<br/>Tiered progression"]
    TYPES --> T2["30 must-know<br/>problems"]
    TIPS --> TI1["Communicate<br/>while coding"]
    TIPS --> TI2["Edge cases<br/>checklist"]
    PRAC --> PR1["LeetCode<br/>HackerRank<br/>NeetCode"]
    classDef root fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef cat  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef leaf fill:#16213e,stroke:#555,color:#ccc
    class INT root
    class PREP,SOLVE,TYPES,TIPS,PRAC cat
    class P1,P2,S1,S2,T1,T2,TI1,TI2,PR1 leaf
```

---

## The UMPIRE Problem-Solving Framework {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
A structured 6-step method for tackling any DSA problem under interview pressure.

::: {.fragment}
| Step | Action |
|---|---|
| **U** — Understand | Restate the problem; ask clarifying questions |
| **M** — Match | Identify pattern / data structure |
| **P** — Plan | Write pseudocode; think aloud |
| **I** — Implement | Code cleanly, name variables well |
| **R** — Review | Trace through example; fix bugs |
| **E** — Evaluate | State time and space complexity |
:::

::: {.fragment}
::: {.callout-tip}
Spend **30–40 % of your time** on U + M + P before writing a single line of code.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    U["🔍 Understand<br/>Clarify constraints,<br/>confirm examples"] --> M["🗂 Match<br/>Which pattern fits?<br/>Which data structure?"]
    M --> P["📝 Plan<br/>Pseudocode first,<br/>think edge cases"]
    P --> I["💻 Implement<br/>Clean code, modular<br/>functions"]
    I --> R["🔄 Review<br/>Trace with example,<br/>fix off-by-ones"]
    R --> E["⏱ Evaluate<br/>State O(time) + O(space)<br/>Discuss trade-offs"]
    classDef step fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    class U,M,P,I,R,E step
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Interview Question Categories {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'flowchart': {'rankSpacing': 50}}}%%
graph LR
    Q["Interview<br/>Question Types"] --> ARR["🟢 Arrays &<br/>Strings"]
    Q --> LL["🟢 Linked<br/>Lists"]
    Q --> STQ["🟡 Stacks &<br/>Queues"]
    Q --> HT["🟡 Hash Maps<br/>& Sets"]
    Q --> TR["🟡 Trees &<br/>BST"]
    Q --> GR["🔴 Graphs"]
    Q --> DP["🔴 Dynamic<br/>Programming"]
    Q --> BS["🟡 Binary<br/>Search"]
    Q --> HP["🟡 Heaps &<br/>Priority Q"]
    Q --> BT["🔴 Backtracking"]
    ARR --> A1["Two Sum · Best Time<br/>to Buy Stock · Kadane"]
    LL  --> L1["Reverse · Merge<br/>Sorted · Cycle Detect"]
    STQ --> SQ1["Valid Parentheses<br/>Min Stack · LRU Cache"]
    HT  --> H1["Group Anagrams<br/>Top K · Subarray Sum=K"]
    TR  --> T1["Max Depth · LCA<br/>Validate BST · Diameter"]
    GR  --> G1["Islands · Clone Graph<br/>Course Schedule · Dijkstra"]
    DP  --> D1["Climb Stairs · House Robber<br/>Coin Change · LCS"]
    BS  --> B1["Search Rotated · Median<br/>of Two Arrays · Koko"]
    HP  --> HP1["K Closest · Task Sched.<br/>Find Median Stream"]
    BT  --> BT1["Subsets · Permutations<br/>Word Search · N-Queens"]
    classDef root fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef cat  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef prb  fill:#16213e,stroke:#555,color:#ccc
    class Q root
    class ARR,LL,STQ,HT,TR,GR,DP,BS,HP,BT cat
    class A1,L1,SQ1,H1,T1,G1,D1,B1,HP1,BT1 prb
```

---

## 30 Must-Know Interview Problems {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Arrays & Strings
| # | Problem | Pattern | Difficulty |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Two Sum | Hash Map | 🟢 Easy |
| 2 | Best Time to Buy & Sell Stock | Sliding Window | 🟢 Easy |
| 3 | Contains Duplicate | Hash Set | 🟢 Easy |
| 4 | Product of Array Except Self | Prefix/Suffix | 🟡 Medium |
| 5 | Maximum Subarray (Kadane) | DP / Greedy | 🟡 Medium |
| 6 | Longest Substring No Repeat | Sliding Window | 🟡 Medium |
| 7 | 3Sum | Two Pointers | 🟡 Medium |
| 8 | Trapping Rain Water | Monotonic Stack | 🔴 Hard |

### Linked Lists
| # | Problem | Pattern | Difficulty |
|---|---|---|---|
| 9 | Reverse Linked List | In-place Reversal | 🟢 Easy |
| 10 | Merge Two Sorted Lists | Two Pointers | 🟢 Easy |
| 11 | Linked List Cycle | Fast & Slow | 🟢 Easy |
| 12 | Remove Nth From End | Fast & Slow | 🟡 Medium |
| 13 | Merge K Sorted Lists | K-way Merge | 🔴 Hard |
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Trees & Graphs
| # | Problem | Pattern | Difficulty |
|---|---|---|---|
| 14 | Maximum Depth of Binary Tree | Tree DFS | 🟢 Easy |
| 15 | Invert Binary Tree | Tree DFS | 🟢 Easy |
| 16 | Validate BST | Tree DFS | 🟡 Medium |
| 17 | LCA of Binary Tree | Tree DFS | 🟡 Medium |
| 18 | Binary Tree Level Order | Tree BFS | 🟡 Medium |
| 19 | Number of Islands | BFS / DFS | 🟡 Medium |
| 20 | Course Schedule | Topological Sort | 🟡 Medium |
| 21 | Clone Graph | BFS / DFS | 🟡 Medium |

### Dynamic Programming
| # | Problem | Pattern | Difficulty |
|---|---|---|---|
| 22 | Climbing Stairs | DP (Fibonacci) | 🟢 Easy |
| 23 | House Robber | DP (1D) | 🟡 Medium |
| 24 | Coin Change | DP (Unbounded KS) | 🟡 Medium |
| 25 | Longest Common Subsequence | DP (2D) | 🟡 Medium |
| 26 | Word Break | DP + Hash Set | 🟡 Medium |
| 27 | Unique Paths | DP (Grid) | 🟡 Medium |
| 28 | Jump Game II | Greedy | 🟡 Medium |
| 29 | Median of Two Sorted Arrays | Binary Search | 🔴 Hard |
| 30 | N-Queens | Backtracking | 🔴 Hard |
:::
:::

---

## Worked Example — Two Sum {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Problem:** Given an array `nums` and `target`, return indices of two numbers that add to `target`. Assume exactly one solution.

::: {.fragment}
**U — Understand:**  
Input: `nums=[2,7,11,15]`, `target=9`  
Output: `[0,1]` (since `2+7=9`)  
Constraints: no same element twice  

**M — Match:** complement lookup → **Hash Map**

**P — Plan:** for each `num`, check if `target - num` seen before
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{.python code-line-numbers="1-8|10-12"}
# O(n) time · O(n) space
def two_sum(nums, target):
    seen = {}                   # val → index
    for i, num in enumerate(nums):
        complement = target - num
        if complement in seen:
            return [seen[complement], i]
        seen[num] = i

nums = [2, 7, 11, 15]
print(two_sum(nums, 9))         # [0, 1]
print(two_sum([3, 2, 4], 6))    # [1, 2]
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    S["i=0  num=2<br/>complement=9-2=7<br/>7 in seen? NO<br/>store seen[2]=0"]
    S --> S2["i=1  num=7<br/>complement=9-7=2<br/>2 in seen? YES ✅<br/>return [seen[2], 1] = [0,1]"]
    classDef step fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef hit  fill:#1a6b3c,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class S step
    class S2 hit
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**E — Evaluate:**

| Approach | Time | Space |
|---|---|---|
| Brute force (nested) | O(n²) | O(1) |
| Sorting + two ptr | O(n log n) | O(1) |
| **Hash map (optimal)** | **O(n)** | **O(n)** |
:::
:::
:::

---

## Worked Example — Maximum Subarray (Kadane's) {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Problem:** Find the contiguous subarray with the **largest sum**.

::: {.fragment}
**U — Understand:**  
Input: `[-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4]`  
Output: `6` (subarray `[4,-1,2,1]`)

**M — Match:** keep running sum, reset if negative → **Greedy / Kadane's DP**

**P — Plan:**  
`curr` = max subarray ending here  
`best` = global max
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{.python code-line-numbers="1-6|8-10"}
def max_subarray(nums):
    curr = best = nums[0]
    for num in nums[1:]:
        curr = max(num, curr + num)  # extend or restart
        best = max(best, curr)
    return best

nums = [-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4]
print(max_subarray(nums))   # 6
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    subgraph "Kadane trace"
        direction TB
        R0["-2  curr=-2  best=-2"]
        R0 --> R1["1   curr=1   best=1"]
        R1 --> R2["-3  curr=-2  best=1"]
        R2 --> R3["4   curr=4   best=4"]
        R3 --> R4["-1  curr=3   best=4"]
        R4 --> R5["2   curr=5   best=5"]
        R5 --> R6["1   curr=6   best=6 ✅"]
        R6 --> R7["-5  curr=1   best=6"]
        R7 --> R8["4   curr=5   best=6"]
    end
    classDef row  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef best fill:#1a6b3c,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class R0,R1,R2,R3,R4,R5,R7,R8 row
    class R6 best
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Worked Example — Validate BST {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Problem:** Given a binary tree root, determine if it is a valid Binary Search Tree.

::: {.fragment}
**U — Understand:**  
Every node must satisfy **min < node.val < max** where the bounds tighten as we recurse.

**M — Match:** recurse with bounds → **Tree DFS with range**

**P — Plan:**  
Pass `(node, min_bound, max_bound)` down. At each node check `min < val < max`, then recurse left with `max=val` and right with `min=val`.
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{.python code-line-numbers="1-9"}
def is_valid_bst(root):
    def dfs(node, lo, hi):
        if not node:
            return True
        if not (lo < node.val < hi):
            return False
        return (dfs(node.left,  lo,       node.val) and
                dfs(node.right, node.val, hi))
    return dfs(root, float('-inf'), float('inf'))
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    R["5<br/>(-∞, +∞) ✅"] --> L["3<br/>(-∞, 5) ✅"]
    R --> RI["7<br/>(5, +∞) ✅"]
    L --> LL["1<br/>(-∞, 3) ✅"]
    L --> LR["4<br/>(3, 5) ✅"]
    RI --> RL["6<br/>(5, 7) ✅"]
    RI --> RR["9<br/>(7, +∞) ✅"]
    classDef ok   fill:#1a6b3c,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef fail fill:#7a1a1a,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class R,L,RI,LL,LR,RL,RR ok
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
::: {.callout-warning}
**Common mistake:** only checking left child < root < right child is **wrong** — a node deep in the left subtree could still violate the global BST property.
:::
:::
:::
:::

---

## Worked Example — Coin Change {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Problem:** Given coin denominations and an `amount`, return the **fewest coins** needed, or `-1` if impossible.

::: {.fragment}
**U — Understand:**  
`coins=[1,5,11]`, `amount=15` → `3` (5+5+5)  
`coins=[2]`, `amount=3` → `-1`

**M — Match:** min coins to reach each sub-amount → **Unbounded Knapsack DP**

**P — Plan:**  
`dp[i]` = minimum coins to make amount `i`.  
For each coin, update `dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[i-coin]+1)`
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{.python code-line-numbers="1-8|10-11"}
def coin_change(coins, amount):
    INF = float('inf')
    dp  = [INF] * (amount + 1)
    dp[0] = 0                       # 0 coins for amount 0
    for coin in coins:
        for i in range(coin, amount + 1):
            dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[i - coin] + 1)
    return dp[amount] if dp[amount] != INF else -1

print(coin_change([1, 5, 11], 15))  # 3  (5+5+5)
print(coin_change([2], 3))          # -1
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    subgraph "dp build for coins=[1,5,11], amount=7"
        direction TB
        D0["dp[0]=0"]
        D1["dp[1]=1"]
        D2["dp[2]=2"]
        D3["dp[3]=3"]
        D4["dp[4]=4"]
        D5["dp[5]=1  (coin 5)"]
        D6["dp[6]=2"]
        D7["dp[7]=3 ✅"]
    end
    classDef base fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef step fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef ans  fill:#1a6b3c,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class D0 base
    class D1,D2,D3,D4,D5,D6 step
    class D7 ans
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Worked Example — Number of Islands {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Problem:** Count connected groups of `'1'`s (land) in a 2D grid of `'1'`s and `'0'`s.

::: {.fragment}
**M — Match:** count connected components → **DFS flood-fill**

**P — Plan:** iterate grid; when `'1'` found, DFS to mark the whole island as visited (`'0'`), increment counter.
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{.python code-line-numbers="1-4|6-15|17-20"}
def num_islands(grid):
    if not grid: return 0
    rows, cols = len(grid), len(grid[0])
    count = 0

    def dfs(r, c):
        if r < 0 or r >= rows: return
        if c < 0 or c >= cols: return
        if grid[r][c] != '1':  return
        grid[r][c] = '0'               # mark visited
        dfs(r+1,c); dfs(r-1,c)
        dfs(r,c+1); dfs(r,c-1)

    for r in range(rows):
        for c in range(cols):
            if grid[r][c] == '1':
                dfs(r, c)              # flood fill
                count += 1
    return count
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    subgraph "Grid"
        direction LR
        G["1 1 0 0 0<br/>1 1 0 0 0<br/>0 0 1 0 0<br/>0 0 0 1 1"]
    end
    I1(["Island 1<br/>top-left cluster"])
    I2(["Island 2<br/>center"])
    I3(["Island 3<br/>bottom-right"])
    G --> I1 & I2 & I3
    ANS(["count = 3"])
    I1 & I2 & I3 --> ANS
    classDef isl fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef ans fill:#1a6b3c,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class I1,I2,I3 isl
    class ANS ans
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**E — Evaluate:**  
Time: **O(m × n)** — each cell visited at most once  
Space: **O(m × n)** — recursion stack in worst case (all land)
:::
:::
:::

---

## Common Interview Mistakes {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Code-Level Mistakes

::: {.fragment}
| Mistake | Fix |
|---|---|
| Off-by-one in binary search | Use `lo <= hi`; `mid = (lo+hi)//2` |
| Forgetting base case in recursion | Always handle `None` / empty first |
| Mutating input array silently | Ask interviewer; work on copy if needed |
| Using list instead of set for membership | `x in set` = O(1) vs `x in list` = O(n) |
| Nested loop when hash map works | Ask: can we trade space for time? |
| Integer overflow (Python exempt) | Note for Java/C++ — use `long` |
| `sort()` vs `sorted()` | `sort()` mutates; `sorted()` returns new |
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Interview-Level Mistakes

::: {.fragment}
| Mistake | Fix |
|---|---|
| Jumping straight to code | Spend time on U + M + P first |
| Silent coding | Talk through every decision |
| No edge-case discussion | Always ask: empty input? negatives? duplicates? |
| Forgetting to state complexity | Always end with O(time) + O(space) |
| One approach only | Mention brute force, then optimise |
| Giving up too fast | Verbalise what you know; ask for hint |
| Untested code | Trace through at least one example |
:::

::: {.fragment}
::: {.callout-tip}
Interviewers care more about **how you think** than whether you get the perfect answer.
:::
:::
:::
:::

---

## Edge Case Checklist {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Arrays / Strings
- Empty array or string
- Single element
- All identical elements
- Already sorted / reverse sorted
- Negative numbers
- Integer overflow (`2^31 - 1`)
- Duplicate values

### Linked Lists
- `None` / empty list
- Single node
- Two nodes (swap edge case)
- Cycle in list
- Head is the answer

### Trees
- `None` root
- Single node (root only)
- Skewed tree (all left or all right)
- All same values
- Negative node values
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Graphs
- Disconnected components
- Self-loops
- Cycle detection
- Directed vs undirected
- Negative edge weights (Dijkstra fails)

### Dynamic Programming
- `amount = 0` (base case)
- `n = 0` or `n = 1`
- No valid solution exists
- Large inputs — check recursion depth

### Strings
- Uppercase + lowercase mixed
- Unicode / special characters
- Palindrome with even/odd length
- Empty string

::: {.fragment}
::: {.callout-warning}
Always **explicitly state** which edge cases you're handling. Even if your code handles them implicitly, verbalising shows thoroughness.
:::
:::
:::
:::

---

## Time & Space Trade-offs {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
Most interview optimisations follow one of three patterns:

::: {.fragment}
### 1. Space for Speed
Use extra memory (hash map, set, prefix array) to reduce time.

```python
# O(n²) → O(n) with hash map
def two_sum_brute(nums, t):        # O(n²)
    for i in range(len(nums)):
        for j in range(i+1, len(nums)):
            if nums[i]+nums[j] == t: return [i,j]

def two_sum_hash(nums, t):         # O(n) time, O(n) space
    seen = {}
    for i, n in enumerate(nums):
        if t - n in seen: return [seen[t-n], i]
        seen[n] = i
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
### 2. Pre-processing
Sort or build prefix/suffix arrays once, then answer queries in O(1).
:::

::: {.fragment}
### 3. Lazy evaluation
Avoid computing things until needed (e.g., short-circuit OR/AND chains).
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    OPT["Optimisation<br/>Strategy"]
    OPT --> SP["🔄 Space-for-Speed<br/>Hash Map · Memo · Prefix Sum<br/>O(n) space → O(n) time"]
    OPT --> PRE["⚙️ Pre-processing<br/>Sort once → binary search<br/>Build index → O(1) query"]
    OPT --> LAZ["⏳ Lazy / Early Exit<br/>Short-circuit conditions<br/>Break on first match"]
    OPT --> DC["✂️ Divide & Reduce<br/>Binary search eliminates half<br/>DP reuses subproblem results"]
    classDef root fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef strat fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    class OPT root
    class SP,PRE,LAZ,DC strat
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Practice Resources & Study Plan {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Recommended Resources

| Resource | Best For |
|---|---|
| **NeetCode 150** | Curated 150 problems by pattern |
| **LeetCode** | Full problem bank, contests |
| **HackerRank** | Structured learning tracks |
| **AlgoExpert** | Video explanations |
| **Grokking the Coding Interview** | Pattern-based learning |
| **CLRS (Introduction to Algorithms)** | Deep theory |
| **Python DSA (this deck!)** | Visual pattern reference |

### Python-specific Tools

```python
import heapq          # priority queue
from collections import (
    deque,            # O(1) both ends
    defaultdict,      # auto-init dict
    Counter,          # frequency count
    OrderedDict,      # insertion order
)
from functools import lru_cache  # memoisation
import bisect         # binary search on sorted list
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### 12-Week Study Plan

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
gantt
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
    title 12-Week DSA Study Plan
    axisFormat  Week %U

    section Foundations
    Big-O + Arrays + Strings    :done,  w1, 2024-01-01, 1w
    Linked Lists + Two Pointers :done,  w2, after w1, 1w
    Stacks + Queues + Sliding W :done,  w3, after w2, 1w

    section Intermediate
    Hash Maps + Sets            :active, w4, after w3, 1w
    Trees + BST + BFS/DFS       :        w5, after w4, 2w
    Heaps + Top-K + Two Heaps   :        w7, after w5, 1w

    section Advanced
    Graphs + BFS/DFS/Topo Sort  :        w8, after w7, 1w
    Binary Search Patterns      :        w9, after w8, 1w
    Dynamic Programming         :        w10, after w9, 2w

    section Mock Interviews
    Backtracking + Revisions    :        w12, after w10, 1w
```
:::
:::

---

## Interview Complexity Quick-Reference {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### "What complexity is expected?"

| Input Size *n* | Expected Complexity |
|---|---|
| n ≤ 10 | O(n!) — backtracking OK |
| n ≤ 20 | O(2ⁿ) — bitmask DP OK |
| n ≤ 100 | O(n³) |
| n ≤ 1,000 | O(n²) |
| n ≤ 100,000 | O(n log n) |
| n ≤ 1,000,000 | O(n) or O(n log n) |
| n > 10,000,000 | O(log n) or O(1) |

::: {.fragment}
::: {.callout-tip}
Use the **input constraint** to reverse-engineer the expected algorithm. If `n ≤ 10^5`, you need O(n log n) or better.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Python Gotchas in Interviews

::: {.fragment}
```python
# ✅ Correct max-heap (negate values)
import heapq
h = []
heapq.heappush(h, -val)
top = -heapq.heappop(h)

# ✅ Avoid TLE: use deque not list for queue
from collections import deque
q = deque(); q.popleft()  # O(1)
# q = []; q.pop(0)        # O(n) ❌

# ✅ sys.setrecursionlimit for deep recursion
import sys; sys.setrecursionlimit(10_000)

# ✅ lru_cache for clean memoisation
from functools import lru_cache
@lru_cache(None)
def dp(i, j): ...

# ✅ Sort by custom key
intervals.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Final Tips — Acing the DSA Interview {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
::: {.fragment}
### Before the Interview
- Solve **2–3 problems/day** in timed conditions
- Review solutions even when correct — are there better approaches?
- Practise on **whiteboard / plain editor** (no autocomplete)
- Mock interview with a partner weekly
:::

::: {.fragment}
### During the Interview
1. **Listen carefully** — constraints are hints
2. **Clarify** before coding (`null` inputs? return type?)
3. **Think out loud** — narrate every step
4. **Start simple** — brute force first, then optimise
5. **Test before claiming done** — trace with examples
6. **State complexity** — always O(time) and O(space)
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    HEAR["Hear problem"] --> RESTATE["Restate in own words"]
    RESTATE --> EDGE["Identify edge cases"]
    EDGE --> BRUTE["State brute-force<br/> complexity"]
    BRUTE --> OPT["Optimise<br/>(pattern match)"]
    OPT --> CODE["Code cleanly"]
    CODE --> TRACE["Trace example"]
    TRACE --> COMPLEX["State O(time)+O(space)"]
    COMPLEX --> IMPROVE["Discuss further improvements"]
    classDef step fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    class HEAR,RESTATE,EDGE,BRUTE,OPT,CODE,TRACE,COMPLEX,IMPROVE step
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
> **"It's not just about getting the right answer — it's about demonstrating clear, structured thinking under pressure."**
:::
:::
:::
